subject: Physics
title: Marie Curie's Glowing Discovery
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Marie Curie's Glowing Discovery.

hook.text: Marie Curie discovered two radioactive elements and became the first person to win two Nobel Prizes.

buildup.text: In 1896, Henri Becquerel found uranium emits invisible rays. Curie decided to investigate further.

discovery.text: She found pitchblende was more radioactive than pure uranium. Something else was hiding inside the ore.

twist.text: She isolated polonium and radium—elements nobody knew existed. Radium glowed eerily in the dark.

climax.text: Curie died from aplastic anemia caused by years of radiation exposure. Her notebooks are still radioactive.

punchline.text: Two elements, two Nobels, and notebooks that still glow.

quiz.question: What two elements did Marie Curie discover?
